Remove the Redis deployment from the production namespace on my OneKE cluster [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Remove Redis deployment from production namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "3 minutes"
  risk_level: high
  tags:
    - redis
    - cleanup
    - production
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Check current Redis deployment status before removal"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.uninstall
    description: "Uninstall Redis Helm release"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    on_failure: abort
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify production namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Redis pods are terminated"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.deploy
      description: "Re-deploy Redis if removal was unintended"
      params:
        chart: redis
        namespace: production
        release_name: redis
        repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

```</s>
instruction: Remove the Redis deployment from the production namespace on my OneKE cluster

Can you deploy an Elasticsearch cluster on my OneKE environment with its own namespace and persistent volumes? [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Elasticsearch on OneKE environment with its own namespace and persistent volumes"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "15 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - elasticsearch
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create elasticsearch namespace"
    params:
      name: elasticsearch
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for Elasticsearch data"
    params:
      name: elasticsearch-data
      namespace: elasticsearch
      size: 30Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Elasticsearch via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: elasticsearch
      namespace: elasticsearch
      release_name: elasticsearch
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: elasticsearch-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Elasticsearch pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: elasticsearch
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=elasticsearch"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get Elasticsearch access URL"
    params:
      service_name: elasticsearch
      namespace: elasticsearch
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Elasticsearch pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Elasticsearch Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: elasticsearch
        namespace: elasticsearch
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the Elasticsearch PVC"
      params:
        name: elasticsearch-data
        namespace: elasticsearch
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the elasticsearch namespace"
      params:
        name: elasticsearch
```</s>
instruction: Can you deploy an Elasticsearch cluster on my OneKE environment with its own namespace and persistent volumes?

Deploy WordPress on my OneKE cluster with a dedicated namespace and persistent storage [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy WordPress on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - wordpress
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create wordpress namespace"
    params:
      name: wordpress
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for WordPress data"
    params:
      name: wordpress-data
      namespace: wordpress
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy WordPress via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
      release_name: wordpress
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: wordpress-data
        service:
          type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for WordPress pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: wordpress
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=wordpress"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the WordPress access URL"
    params:
      service_name: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify WordPress pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove WordPress Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: wordpress
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc

```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up Prometheus monitoring with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "8 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - prometheus
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create monitoring namespace"
    params:
      name: monitoring
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Prometheus data"
    params:
      name: prometheus-data
      namespace: monitoring
      size: 50Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Prometheus via Helm"
    params:
      chart: prometheus
      namespace: monitoring
      release_name: prometheus
      repo_url: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        server:
          persistentVolume:
            existingClaim: prometheus-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Prometheus to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: monitoring
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Prometheus web UI via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: prometheus-server
      namespace: monitoring
      port: 9090
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Prometheus is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Prometheus service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Prometheus"
      params:
        release_name: prometheus
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Prometheus storage"
      params:
        name: prometheus-data
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove monitoring namespace"
      params:
        name: monitoring

```</s>

Please assist me in creating a namespace named analytics, deploying MongoDB with a 40Gi volume, and ensuring the database is exposed. [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up MongoDB for analytics with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "9 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - analytics
    - mongodb
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create analytics namespace"
    params:
      name: analytics
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for MongoDB data"
    params:
      name: mongodb-storage
      namespace: analytics
      size: 40Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy MongoDB using Helm"
    params:
      chart: mongodb
      namespace: analytics
      release_name: mongodb
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: mongodb-storage
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for MongoDB to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: analytics
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=mongodb"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose MongoDB service via ClusterIP"
    params:
      deployment_name: mongodb
      namespace: analytics
      port: 27017
      service_type: ClusterIP
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Check cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure MongoDB pod is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Confirm MongoDB service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove MongoDB"
      params:
        release_name: mongodb
        namespace: analytics
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete MongoDB storage"
      params:
        name: mongodb-storage
        namespace: analytics
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove analytics namespace"
      params:
        name: analytics
```</s>

    description: "Create neo4j namespace"
    params:
      name: neo4j
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for Neo4j data"
    params:
      name: neo4j-data
      namespace: neo4j
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Neo4j via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: neo4j
      namespace: neo4j
      release_name: neo4j
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: neo4j-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Neo4j pods to be ready"
    params:

      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=neo4j"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint

      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"

    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Neo4j pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall

    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the Neo4j PVC"
      params:
        name: neo4j-data
        namespace: neo4j
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the neo4j namespace"
      params:
        name: neo4j
```</s>

metadata:
  description: "Create MySQL namespace and deploy on OneKE with persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster

steps:

    - id: step_91

Would you please deploy a RabbitMQ instance on my OneKE cluster with a new namespace and storage? [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy RabbitMQ on OneKE cluster with a new namespace and storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "9 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - rabbitmq
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create rabbitmq namespace"
    params:
      name: rabbitmq
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for RabbitMQ data"
    params:
      name: rabbitmq-data
      namespace: rabbitmq
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy RabbitMQ via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: rabbitmq
      namespace: rabbitmq
      release_name: rabbitmq
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: rabbitmq-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for RabbitMQ pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: rabbitmq
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=rabbitmq"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get RabbitMQ access URL"
    params:
      service_name: rabbitmq
      namespace: rabbitmq
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify RabbitMQ pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove RabbitMQ Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: rabbitmq
        namespace: rabbitmq
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the RabbitMQ PVC"
      params:
        name: rabbitmq-data
        namespace: rabbitmq
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the rabbitmq namespace"
      params:
        name: rabbitmq
```</s>
instruction: Would you please deploy a RabbitMQ instance on my OneKE cluster with a new namespace and storage?

    description: "Create application namespace"

Deploy Grafana on my OneKE cluster with a new namespace and storage configuration [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Grafana on OneKE cluster with namespace and storage configuration"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "9 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - grafana
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create grafana namespace"
    params:
      name: grafana
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for Grafana data"
    params:
      name: grafana-data
      namespace: grafana
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: grafana
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: grafana-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: grafana
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get Grafana access URL"
    params:
      service_name: grafana
      namespace: grafana
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Grafana pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: grafana
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the Grafana PVC"
      params:
        name: grafana-data
        namespace: grafana
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the grafana namespace"
      params:
        name: grafana
```</s>

Set up a namespace named metrics, deploy Grafana with a 20Gi volume, and make its dashboard accessible. [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Establish Grafana metrics visualization with dedicated namespace, storage, and public access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "7 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - grafana
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create metrics namespace"
    params:
      name: metrics
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Grafana"
    params:
      name: grafana-data
      namespace: metrics
      size: 20Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Grafana via Helm"
    params:
      chart: grafana
      namespace: metrics
      release_name: grafana
      repo_url: https://grafana.org/helm-charts
      values:
        persistence:
          enabled: true
          existingClaim: grafana-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Grafana to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: metrics
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Grafana dashboard via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: grafana
      namespace: metrics
      port: 3000
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Grafana is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Grafana service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Grafana"
      params:
        release_name: grafana
        namespace: metrics
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Grafana storage"
      params:
        name: grafana-data
        namespace: metrics
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove metrics namespace"
      params:
        name: metrics
```</s>
instruction: Set up a namespace named metrics, deploy Grafana with a 20Gi volume, and make its dashboard accessible.

Launch a PostgreSQL database on my OneKE cluster with its own namespace and storage. [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy PostgreSQL on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "13 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - postgresql
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create postgresql namespace"
    params:
      name: postgresql
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for PostgreSQL data"
    params:
      name: postgresql-data
      namespace: postgresql
      size: 20Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy PostgreSQL via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: postgresql
      namespace: postgresql
      release_name: postgresql
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: postgresql-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for PostgreSQL pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: postgresql
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=postgresql"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the PostgreSQL access URL"
    params:
      service_name: postgresql
      namespace: postgresql
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify PostgreSQL pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove PostgreSQL Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: postgresql
        namespace: postgresql
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the PostgreSQL PVC"
      params:
        name: postgresql-data
        namespace: postgresql
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the postgresql namespace"
      params:
        name: postgresql
```</s>
instruction: Launch a PostgreSQL database on my OneKE cluster with its own namespace and storage.

version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy WordPress on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - wordpress
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01

Please create a namespace named analytics, deploy MongoDB with a 30Gi volume, and expose its service endpoint. [/INST]
```yaml
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy MongoDB in a dedicated analytics namespace with external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "9 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - database
    - mongodb
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create analytics namespace"
    params:
      name: analytics
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for MongoDB"
    params:
      name: mongodb-data
      namespace: analytics
      size: 30Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy MongoDB via Helm"
    params:
      chart: mongodb
      namespace: analytics
      release_name: mongodb
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: mongodb-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for MongoDB to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: analytics
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=mongodb"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose MongoDB service"
    params:
      deployment_name: mongodb
      namespace: analytics
      port: 27017
      service_type: ClusterIP
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify MongoDB is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify MongoDB service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove MongoDB"
      params:
        release_name: mongodb
        namespace: analytics
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete MongoDB storage"
      params:
        name: mongodb-data
        namespace: analytics
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove analytics namespace"
      params:
        name: analytics
```</s>
instruction: Please create a namespace named analytics, deploy MongoDB with a 30Gi volume, and expose its service endpoint.

version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy Redis in a test namespace with internal access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "5 minutes"
  risk_level: low

    - test
    - redis
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create test namespace"
    params:
      name: test
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Redis"
    params:
      name: redis-storage
      namespace: test
      size: 10Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Redis using Helm"
    params:
      chart: redis
      namespace: test
      release_name: redis
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: redis-storage
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Redis to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: test
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=redis"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Redis service as a ClusterIP"

      namespace: test
      port: 6379
      service_type: ClusterIP
    depends_on:

      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Ensure Redis is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check Redis service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Redis deployment"
      params:
        release_name: redis
        namespace: test
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Redis storage"
      params:
        name: redis-storage
        namespace: test
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete test namespace"
      params:
        name: test
```</s>
